sections.paragraph:
m. with suff נִגְעוּ pl. נְגָעִים, נִגְעֵי.

(1) stroke, blow, Deu 17:8 21:5 in sing. coll. Pro 6:33. Specially of strokes or calamities which God inflicts upon men, Gen 12:17 Exo 11:1 Psa 38:12 39:11 91:10 .

(2) a mark, or spot in the skin, whether a scab, or eruption, or leprosy, Lev 13:3 (compare verse Lev 13:2), Lev 13:5, Lev 13:6, 29 Lev 13:29, 30 Lev 13:30, 42 Lev 13:42whence נֶגַע הַנֶּתֶק a spot of scab, verse 31 Lev 13:31נֶגַע הַצַּרַעַת a spot of leprosy, verses Lev 13:3, Lev 13:9, 20 Lev 13:20, 25 Lev 13:25, and without צָרַעַת verse 22 Lev 13:22, id.; also used of leprosy of garments, Lev 13:47 and of works, Lev 14:34, seq. Meton. used of a man affected with spots, verses Lev 14:4, 12 Lev 14:12, 13 Lev 14:13, 17 Lev 14:17, 31 Lev 14:31and even of leprosy in a garment, verse 50 Lev 14:50.
